parsed_review.summary: CP00-248 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (search-core RP07.P03.M06.S01-RP07.P04.M03.S08, 150 units, risk C) that closes the RP07.P03 interface tail and opens the RP07.P04 UI foundation as nine descriptor sections. Read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim is supported and correctly scoped: manifest.json and command-evidence.json both declare production_ready=false with status "implemented_pending_review", so no enterprise-trust or final-approval claim is being made at this stage. All no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in code (freezeCp248Result, SEARCH_CORE_CP248_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION) with every runtime/write/storage/Hermes-receipt flag false; a grep for runtime/IO/network patterns surfaced only descriptor boundary flags, confirming no executable runtime, persistence, or real-data path. Unit/deliverable/phase/micro/section counts are internally consistent (sum to 150; security_audit=16, fixture=2, claude_review=10 hand-verified) and enforced by validateSearchCoreCp248Coverage. Claude is held to read-only reviewer with claude_is_final_approval=false and tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob; the validate script deep-equals the contract projection against freshly computed descriptors (drift-proof), and model.test.js asserts the descriptor-only and leak boundaries. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets/receipts (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. The CP178+ hardening blocklist (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing-field/trust/approval claims) is present. Local validation commands are recorded as passing in command-evidence.json (exit_code 0); these were not re-run under this read-only review. One cosmetic, non-blocking metadata observation noted. No P0/P1/P2 findings; final human approval remains required outside this receipt.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json classifies the pack's own evidence directory under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-248/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 95-98) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105). The pack's own manifest/evidence directory is not 'unrelated' to CP00-248, so the dual-listing reads as a classification inconsistency. This may instead be intended generator semantics where 'preserved' means 'left unstaged by the scoping step' rather than 'unrelated to the pack'. Either way it concerns input tooling metadata only and has no bearing on the descriptor-only boundaries, the 150-unit counts, the authority boundary, or the production_ready=false claim.
findings.recommendation: Clarify the scope-generator semantics so the pack's own evidence directory is not labeled 'unrelated', or rename the field to reflect 'dirty files preserved/unstaged'. No code or boundary change required; does not block pack or goal closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking observation on input metadata classification only; pack closeout may proceed. Final human approval still required per the authority boundary.
